title: 21.2 — Overloading the arithmetic operators using friend functions

content.code: I have 14 cents.
Result: (3, 16)

content.text: We still haveFraction operator*(const Fraction& f1, const Fraction& f2)When we multiply a fraction with an integer, for exampleFraction f5{ 2 * f2 };The non-explicitFraction(int, int)constructor will be used to construct a newFractionfrom 2. This newFractionis then multiplied byf2using theFraction * Fractionoperator.Because this requires converting2to aFraction, this is slightly slower than the implementation with overloaded operators for integer multiplication.

content.code: Fraction operator*(const Fraction& f1, const Fraction& f2)

content.code: Fraction f5{ 2 * f2 };

content.text: The non-explicitFraction(int, int)constructor will be used to construct a newFractionfrom 2. This newFractionis then multiplied byf2using theFraction * Fractionoperator.Because this requires converting2to aFraction, this is slightly slower than the implementation with overloaded operators for integer multiplication.
Because this requires converting2to aFraction, this is slightly slower than the implementation with overloaded operators for integer multiplication.
We’re multiplying temporaryFractionobjects, but non-const references cannot bind to temporaries.
